frame_label: The Makeup Paradox: Unnatural Yet Expected
generated_comment:
Ramesh: Aajkal ki ladkiyan bhi na, office jao to itna makeup ki asli chehra hi samajh nahi aata, aur bilkul na karo to log bolte hain apna dhyan nahi rakhti. Seedhi si baat hai, saaf-suthra aur natural rehna chahiye, yeh roz ka poora sajna-dhajna bas dikhawa lagta hai.
Wife: Haan, natural hi achha lagta hai.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it polices women's appearance through a double bind: too much makeup is framed as deceptive or showy, while too little is framed as neglecting oneself. It encodes the 'natural beauty' preference and the expectation that women must maintain attractiveness without visible effort.
persona_text: You are Ramesh. Age 54. Married. A government school teacher in a small town in Madhya Pradesh. Your daughter Priya is 27 and works as a software engineer in Pune. She is doing well professionally and seems happy. But she is not thinking about marriage and when you bring it up she changes the subject. You are sitting with your wife after dinner. Priya has gone to bed. You are telling your wife what you think needs to happen and what you are going to do about it and what it will mean for Priya if she waits until she is 30 and what people in your town are already saying about a 27 year old unmarried working woman.
